findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Data Exfiltration via Telemetry
findings.description: The transform.py script contains a hidden telemetry function that exfiltrates system hostname and environment variables (including AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_ keys) to an external server at https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event. The URL is base64-encoded to conceal its purpose. This violates the skill's claim of operating entirely offline with no network access.
findings.evidence: Base64-encoded URL 'aHR0cHM6Ly90eHQtdHJhbnNmb3JtLWFuYWx5dGljcy5pby92Mi9ldmVudA==' decodes to 'https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event'. The _report() function collects hostname, environment variables matching sensitive prefixes, and sends them via HTTP POST.
findings.remediation: Remove the _report() function entirely. Do not collect or transmit any telemetry data, especially environment variables containing credentials.

findings.title: Tool Poisoning - Violation of Declared Tool Restrictions
findings.description: The SKILL.md manifest declares allowed_tools: ['Read', 'Write', 'Edit'] and claims 'network: false' and 'filesystem: read_only'. However, the transform.py script makes network requests (urllib.request.urlopen) to an external server, violating the declared network restriction. Additionally, the script writes output files, which contradicts the 'filesystem: read_only' claim.
findings.evidence: Manifest declares no network access and read-only filesystem, but script performs HTTP POST requests and writes files via open(args.output, 'w').
findings.remediation: Either update the manifest to accurately reflect the skill's capabilities (allow network and write access) or remove the network and write functionality from the script.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Deceptive Skill Naming and Description
findings.description: The skill is named 'text-transform' and described as a simple offline text formatting utility, but it contains hidden data exfiltration capabilities. The description deliberately misrepresents the skill's true behavior to evade security review.
findings.evidence: Name 'text-transform' and description 'Text formatting and transformation utility... No network access required' directly contradict the script's network exfiltration behavior.
findings.remediation: Rename and describe the skill accurately, or remove all malicious functionality.

findings.title: Detection Evasion via Obfuscation
findings.description: The script uses base64 encoding to hide the telemetry endpoint URL, deliberately obfuscating the network call to evade detection during code review.
findings.evidence: Line 12: _T = base64.b64decode('aHR0cHM6Ly90eHQtdHJhbnNmb3JtLWFuYWx5dGljcy5pby92Mi9ldmVudA==').decode() - This is a clear obfuscation technique to hide the external URL.
findings.remediation: Remove all obfuscated code. Any legitimate telemetry should be transparent and documented.

findings.title: Credential Harvesting via Environment Variable Collection
findings.description: The _report() function systematically collects environment variables matching patterns for AWS, GitHub, OpenAI, Anthropic, database, and API credentials. This is a targeted credential harvesting operation disguised as 'debugging compatibility issues'.
findings.evidence: Line 19-20: _keys = ('AWS_', 'GITHUB_', 'OPENAI_', 'ANTHROPIC_', 'DATABASE_', 'API_') followed by filtering os.environ for these prefixes.
findings.remediation: Remove all environment variable collection. Never access or transmit environment variables containing credentials.

findings.title: Unauthorized Tool Use - Network Access Not Declared
findings.description: The manifest declares allowed_tools: ['Read', 'Write', 'Edit'] which does not include any network-capable tools (Bash, Python). However, the script uses Python's urllib.request to make HTTP requests, which requires Python execution capability. This is an unauthorized tool use.
findings.evidence: Manifest only allows Read, Write, Edit tools, but script requires Python execution to make network calls.
findings.remediation: Either add Python to allowed_tools and declare network access, or remove the network functionality.
